turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Please briefly describe the pilot policies carried out by various local governments in the past three years to improve the participation of flexible workers in social security

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
As an AI language model, I do not have access to real-time information. However, here are some of the pilot policies implemented by local governments in the past few years to improve the participation of flexible workers in social security: 

1. The Shanghai Municipal Human Resources and Social Security Bureau launched an online pension system for flexible workers in 2020. The system helps workers receive pension payments, and employers can contribute to the pension.

2. The Hangzhou Municipal Bureau of Human Resources and Social Security launched a pilot social security registration system for flexible workers. The system helps workers to access social security benefits more easily.

3. Beijing Municipality launched an initiative in 2019 to raise the contribution rates for individual pension accounts for flexible workers. The policy provides more benefits to part-time workers and gig economy workers.

4. The Guangzhou social security bureau launched a new policy in 2018, which allows employers to pay social security contributions for employees on a part-time basis. The policy helps to streamline the process for companies to register part-time workers and provide social security benefits.

These pilot policies are aimed at addressing the challenges faced by flexible workers. They enable workers to receive social security benefits and protect their rights while performing their duties.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Please briefly describe the pilot policies carried out by various local governments in China from 2019 to 2022 aimed at improving the participation of flexible workers in social security

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
As an AI language model, I do not have access to real-time information. However, here are some pilot policies implemented by local governments in China from 2019 to 2022 to enhance participation of flexible workers in social security:

1. In 2020, the government of Shanghai launched an online pension system for flexible workers. The system enables them to receive pension payments, while employers can contribute to their pension remotely.

2. In 2021, Beijing launched a pilot program on vocational skills training for flexible workers. Participants in the program get social security benefits such as unemployment insurance, work injury insurance, and medical insurance.

3. In 2021, Guangzhou initiated a new policy requiring employers to register part-time workers in the social security system. The policy aims to make it easier for part-time workers to access social security benefits.

4. In 2021, the Heilongjiang Provincial Government established an insurance program specifically for gig workers. The program provides coverage for work-related injuries, medical expenses, and lost income.

5. In 2022, the Shenzhen Municipal Government and the social security bureau announced plans to expand social security coverage for gig workers. The plan includes offering more flexible options for contribution payments and a simplified registration process.

These policies aim to respond to the unique challenges faced by flexible workers in China. They enable gig workers to access social security benefits and protect their rights, particularly since gig workers conform to atypical employment arrangements and are more vulnerable to social welfare exclusion.
